entity.name: Mohd Kaif
entity.description:
AI engineer and lead maintainer of Semantica. I left my job to focus entirely on this project after recognising a clear and unmet need in the open-source ecosystem: developers building AI systems had no solid, production-ready framework for semantic reasoning, knowledge graphs, and explainable decision tracking.

Over the past year I have shipped 29 production-ready modules, 50+ Jupyter notebook tutorials, integrations with FAISS, Neo4j, AWS Neptune, Apache AGE, and Docling, and a complete W3C PROV-O compliant provenance layer — all without any external funding. Everything has been self-funded.

This grant would allow me to continue as lead maintainer for 12 months while bringing in part-time contributors — engineers, a technical writer, and domain experts — to scale the project beyond what a single person can sustain. The goal is to transition Semantica from a solo project into a small, productive open-source team.

projects.name: Semantica
projects.description:
Semantica is a free, MIT-licensed Python framework that gives AI applications a proper semantic layer — the missing piece between raw text and trustworthy AI.

The problem: Modern AI systems (RAG pipelines, agents, multi-agent workflows) treat knowledge as bags of text chunks. They have no understanding of entities, relationships, rules, or decision history. In high-stakes domains — healthcare, finance, legal, cybersecurity — this leads to black-box answers that cannot be explained, audited, or trusted.

What Semantica does: It provides a complete, end-to-end pipeline from raw data to governed, explainable AI output:
• Universal ingestion — 50+ file formats (PDF, DOCX, HTML, CSV, databases, APIs)
• Semantic extraction — named entity recognition, relationship extraction, event detection
• Knowledge graph construction — with conflict detection, deduplication, and entity resolution
• Automatic ontology generation — 6-stage LLM pipeline producing OWL ontologies
• GraphRAG — hybrid vector + graph retrieval for 30%+ accuracy gains over plain RAG
• Decision tracking — full lifecycle management with precedent search, causal analysis, and policy compliance
• Provenance tracking — W3C PROV-O compliant lineage from source document to AI response
• Change management — versioned knowledge graphs with SHA-256 integrity verification

What makes it different: Semantica is not another wrapper around LangChain. It is the semantic intelligence layer that sits beneath your agents and makes them auditable, explainable, and trustworthy. It integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, CrewAI, and other frameworks rather than replacing them.

Traction: Available on PyPI, actively downloaded, MIT-licensed, 29 production-ready modules, 50+ tutorial notebooks spanning 14 real-world use-case domains (biomedical, finance, cybersecurity, supply chain, government, autonomous systems).

This funding will sustain full-time development for 12 months, enabling API stabilisation, expanded integrations, performance improvements, and community growth.

channels.address: Email-only: Contact kaifahmad087@gmail.com to receive bank account details securely.
channels.description: Bank transfers accepted. Email kaifahmad087@gmail.com to receive full account details and instructions.
Support Semantica through GitHub Sponsors with recurring or one-time payments.

plans.name: FLOSS/fund Grant — 12 Months Full-Time Development
plans.description:
One-time grant to sustain 12 months of open-source development of Semantica, transitioning from a solo project to a small, collaborative open-source team.

Budget breakdown:

— People —
• Lead maintainer stipend (12 months) — $26,000: Reduced stipend allowing full-time focus. Deliberately lean to leave room for contributors.
• Part-time engineer #1 (backend / KG algorithms, ~10 hrs/week, 12 months) — $7,200: Expands coverage of graph algorithms, performance optimisation, and integration testing across the 29 modules.
• Part-time engineer #2 (integrations / vector stores, ~8 hrs/week, 12 months) — $5,760: Owns new vector store and LLM provider integrations (Weaviate, Qdrant, ChromaDB, Anthropic, Gemini).
• Part-time technical writer / community manager (~6 hrs/week, 12 months) — $3,600: Maintains documentation quality, writes onboarding guides, manages Discord and GitHub Discussions, coordinates contributor onboarding.
• Open bounty pool for community contributors — $2,440: Paid bounties for community PRs covering bug fixes, new domain cookbooks, translations, and test coverage improvements. Distributed via GitHub Issues bounty labels.

Total people: $45,000

— Infrastructure & tooling —
• CI/CD runners, GPU compute, cloud database instances (Neptune, Neo4j Aura) — $3,000
• Paid API credits (OpenAI, Groq, Anthropic) for automated integration tests — $2,000

Total infrastructure: $5,000

Grand total: $50,000 USD

Deliverables over 12 months:
1. Stable v1.0 release with frozen public API and migration guides
2. Full test coverage (unit + integration) across all 29 modules — led by part-time engineers
3. Performance benchmarks: GraphRAG vs. plain RAG across 5 domains, published openly
4. Expanded integrations: Weaviate, Qdrant, ChromaDB, Pinecone; Anthropic and Gemini LLM providers — owned by part-time integration engineer
5. Healthcare and finance compliance guides (HIPAA, SOX) with working code examples
6. 10+ community-contributed cookbooks via bounty programme
7. Active, self-sustaining community: Discord, GitHub Discussions, monthly release notes

Why this structure: A distributed part-time team is more resilient than one full-time solo maintainer. Reducing the lead maintainer stipend to fund contributors means the project scales faster, knowledge is spread across multiple people, and the community has a clear path to contributing paid work. This is a deliberate choice for long-term sustainability.

plans.name: Core Development Support (Monthly Sponsors)
plans.description: Monthly sponsorship to support ongoing full-time development of Semantica. Covers maintainer time, infrastructure, and tooling on a sustainable recurring basis.

plans.name: Infrastructure Support (Monthly Sponsors)
plans.description: Covers hosting, CI/CD, cloud database instances, GPU compute for tests, and automation pipelines used in development and integration testing.

history.description: Fully self-funded. No external grants, investors, or sponsorships. All development carried out using personal savings after leaving employment to work on Semantica full-time.
